note: V2: 5 categories (value/promo/thread/engagement/seasonal). 80 tweets total. ~27 day cycle. Auto-reset when a category is exhausted.

tweets.text:
The Eisenhower Matrix in 30 seconds:

Urgent + Important = Do it now
Important, not urgent = Schedule it
Urgent, not important = Delegate it
Neither = Delete it

90% of your stress comes from treating everything as box 1.

#Productivity

Freelancer reality check:

If you bill 40 hrs/week but spend 15 hrs on admin, your real rate is 62% of what you think.

Track your non-billable hours. The number will shock you.

#FreelanceTips

The human brain can hold 4-7 items in working memory.

Your to-do list has 47.

Write. Everything. Down.

Not in your head. Not on sticky notes. In ONE system you trust.

#SecondBrain #Productivity

Sunday habit that changed my life:

1. Clear all inboxes (5 min)
2. Review last week's wins (3 min)
3. Pick 3 priorities for next week (5 min)
4. Move stalled tasks to "Someday" (2 min)

15 minutes. Entire week feels different.

#ProductivityTips

Stop creating content day-by-day.

Monday: Write 5 posts
Tuesday: Design all graphics
Wednesday: Schedule the week
Thursday-Sunday: Engage

Batch creation. Daily distribution. Stress drops 80%.

#ContentCreator

I tracked my subscriptions last month:

- Todoist: $5
- Trello: $10
- Airtable: $20
- Calendly: $12
- Notion: $0

Guess which one replaced the other four.

#Notion

Hot take: Most side hustles are negative hourly rate.

Revenue sounds great until you subtract:
- Tools/subscriptions
- Taxes set aside
- Hours actually worked

Know your REAL $/hour before calling it "passive income."

#SideHustle

The students getting A's aren't smarter.

They just track:
- Every assignment + due date
- Study hours per subject
- Grade trends over time

When you see the data, you know exactly where to focus.

#StudyTips

You don't lose clients because your work is bad.

You lose clients because you forgot to follow up.

A follow-up system beats talent every single time.

#FreelanceTips #SmallBusiness

The problem with New Year goals:

- Written on January 1
- Forgotten by February
- No tracking system
- No weekly check-in

Goals without a system are just wishes.

#Productivity

I deleted 9 apps from my phone last month.

Replaced them all with one workspace on my laptop.

Less context switching = more deep work = better output.

#DigitalMinimalism #Productivity

I tracked 200+ job applications in a spreadsheet.

Key insight: The jobs I got interviews for had one thing in common — I customized the first sentence of my cover letter.

Data reveals patterns your gut never will.

#JobSearch

Read 20 books this year and remember nothing?

Try this:
- 3 key takeaways per chapter
- 1 action item per book
- Review notes monthly

Retention goes from 10% to 70%.

#Reading #SecondBrain

As a solopreneur, your most dangerous habit is "busy work."

Checking email 30 times isn't working.
Reorganizing your desktop isn't working.
47 browser tabs isn't working.

Ruthlessly protect deep work blocks.

#Solopreneur

Social media tip that took me too long to learn:

Plan content in categories, not chronologically.

- 40% educational
- 30% personal/story
- 20% promotional
- 10% engagement

A system beats inspiration every time.

#ContentCreator

Want to build a habit? Attach it to one you already have.

"After I pour my morning coffee, I write 3 priorities for the day."

One sentence. Life-changing compound effect over 365 days.

#HabitStacking #Productivity

Freelancer tip: Send your invoice the SAME DAY you finish the work.

Every day you delay = lower chance of getting paid on time.

Set up a "days since invoiced" tracker. Automate the follow-up.

#FreelanceTips

The best productivity system is the one you actually use.

Not the prettiest. Not the most complex. Not the one your favorite YouTuber uses.

The one YOU open every single day.

#Notion #Productivity

Decision fatigue is why you eat junk food at 9 PM.

You made 35,000 decisions today. Your brain is fried.

Solution: Automate the small decisions. Meal prep. Outfit rotation. Pre-planned content calendar.

Save brainpower for what matters.

#Productivity

1% better every day = 37x better in a year.

But only if you track it.

You can't improve what you don't measure.

#Productivity #CompoundEffect

How to set up your entire life system in 30 minutes:

1. Rate 8 life areas 1-10 (5 min)

How to audit your side hustle in 15 minutes:

1. Write down ALL income (last 3 months)
2. Write down ALL expenses
3. Estimate total HOURS/month
4. Calculate: (Revenue - Expenses) / Hours = Real $/hour
5. Compare to your day job rate

If it pays less than your 9-5... you have a hobby, not a business.
